summary:
The patient presents with signs indicative of bile acid malabsorption alongside elevated conjugated bilirubin levels; coupled with hepato-cellular accumulation visualized by specific histochemical methods pointing towards a condition involving excessive metal ion retention such as Wilson’s disease where abnormal protein-copper complexes lead to tissue injury manifesting microscopically into distinctive cytoplasmic aggregates identified under periodic-acid-Schiff reaction. Thus,

The most likely cause for this infant's findings appears to be \boxed{\textbf{Wilson's Disease}} given its unique combination of biochemical abnormalities together with pathognomonic inclusion bodies observed post-PAS staining procedure upon examination from biopsied tissues. To confirm further diagnostic workup would include ceruloplasmin level measurement among others typically recommended when suspecting Wilson's syndrome clinically based solely off these initial clues
